Fund: HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund
Category: ELSS (Tax Savings)
Riskometer: Very High Risk
This is an HDFC mutual fund classified as a ELSS (Tax Savings). The fund carries a Very High Risk rating on the riskometer, indicating the level of risk associated with investing in this fund. Source: https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/hdfc-elss-taxsaver-direct-plan-growth-option-2685

Fund: HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund
Category: ELSS (Tax Savings)
NAV (Net Asset Value): ₹0.00 as of March 11, 2026
Currency: INR
The current Net Asset Value (NAV) of HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund is ₹0.00. NAV represents the per-unit market value of the fund and is updated daily after market close (3:30 PM IST on trading days).

Fund: HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund
Category: ELSS (Tax Savings)
Expense Ratio: 1.08%
Exit Load: 0%
The expense ratio of HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund is 1.08%. This is the annual fee charged by the fund house to manage your investment. There is no exit load for HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund.

Fund: HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund
Category: ELSS (Tax Savings)
Minimum SIP: Not specified
Lock-in Period: Minimum investment for lump sum payment is INR 500.00 and for SIP is INR 500.00. There is a lock in period for HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund of 3 Years
The minimum SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) amount for HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund is Not specified. HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund has a mandatory lock-in period of Minimum investment for lump sum payment is INR 500.00 and for SIP is INR 500.00. There is a lock in period for HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund of 3 Years. This is typical for ELSS funds, which qualify for tax deductions under Section 80C.
